title: Level 741
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 741: "The Kafkaesque"}} [[Category:Levels]][[Category:Cluster I]][[Category:Format Screws]][[Category:Infohazard]] {{User:Papashfh/770 title}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair Display'); html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family:Playfair Display }} {{Collapse|num=hidden|initial=Toggle Audio|final=Toggle Audio|body={{Audio|file=Back there Benjamin.mp3|align=center|volume=1|loop=true|=yes}}}} "Fuck you" were the first words to stumble out of his mouth after such a mockery. The rule of thumb was to not do that, but he just so happened to do it. Why? How could he do such an act? But the world will keep spinning. Every single clock on the wall, every single hand will continue—if they weren't broken from the start. The person on the other side of the table could not do anything more than just chuckle. But the chuckles grew too loud and gave way to horrific cackling. Banging his hands on the table so hard it could be heard from the other room. "Stop." Who said that? No more than poor old Benjamin. The same innocent soul who dared to say a bad word. But the man on the other side of the table could not stop. "You see, Benjamin, it all started from here and went there. 'But oh, from where?' you will ask. From back there, Benjamin." "I don't understand."
Excerpt from ''Back there, Benjamin'' (Elaine Sullivan)
---- ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial=
?
|final=|body= {{Collapse|initial=
It has been a long time since existing. You know nothing of your purpose, nothing of who you are, only to keep going forward, and a part of some story. Why do you suffer this fate? There was a radio on the table. To you, it was just a weird-looking object with buttons on it. With the press of one, noise started coming out of it: ----
[[File:Back There, Benjamin.jpg|600px|center|link=]]
''fig 1.'' Level upon arrival



"Your fate was set upon opening the door. The door would suck you in before the brain could even notice the environment shifting. Oddly, this would be a line that would be heard about an [[Level 87|inescapable paradise]], but the place I was referring to is quite escapable. Believe me or not having a guaranteed exit, if it were not for the effects the place has on minds. The area has an infohazard tied to it. Even if mentioned slightly anywhere, it would be replaced by a snippet from "Back there, Benjamin," a book written by Elaine Sullivan. For the sake of an example, if you would casually mention the level, instead of your original words coming out, the excerpt would spew out. The phenomenon causes the level to self-redact. What I am likely telling you has been heard as the excerpt. From this, it is almost impossible to document with the given memory loss. No book called that exists. The level likely made it up, but why? Upon arrival, the hippocampus would weaken. At first, the symptoms are subtle on the scale of what is bound to happen: everything before the initial entry would be forgotten. Of course, muscle memory will remain the same. The only forgotten thing would be your own self, which makes who you are, what makes you a meatball with emotions. Your family, your wife or husband, your kids, your pets, your everything won't be missed. Even your name will get swallowed into the pit of forgetfulness. The most you will be able to do is walk, talk, and perform other basic skills that one would learn at a young age. The memories will be replaced with the same excerpt that the level redact itself with. The forgetfulness becomes worse and worse until you forget what you did the previous second. Your head will be empty, and your mind will be blank. It will feel like something is on the tip of your tongue, but it never will be; it was your last memory you forgot: your name. You will mutter out the words "who am I," but nobody will dare to remember. The only things that will not be forgotten, aside from basic abilities, are thoughts. They will be clearer and remembered for way longer. This is from them manifesting physically as floating text, breaking the rules of space. Your thoughts will be able to manipulate the environment, so use them wisely; that may help you. But be careful not to change reality too much, otherwise you will end up [[The Void|there]]. It has been a long time since I told you about where you are positioned without explaining the place. It feels appropriate to mention it by this point. The place is a blend of a basilica and a church, likely inspired by early Byzantine churches. There are mosaic paintings on the walls depicting false biblical stories, bearing blended, meshed colors. Each basilica is connected through the entrance, which would have formed a maze of sorts. Windows appear high up, sending false sunlight through. Leaving here is considerably easier than most places you once thrived in. Take shelter in the level for 6 months, and wherever you go, there will always be a corridor in front of you. It has a door at the end that sends you back to [[Level 0|the yellow office]], restoring all your memories except for the time spent here. The thing that makes this hard is that you may forget the concept of time." |num=2|final=|body= {{Collapse|initial=
The message did not go through. Or well, it did. But it was heard as something else.
|final= |num=03 |body= "Fuck you" were the first words to stumble out of his mouth after such a mockery. The rule of thumb was to not do that, but he just so happened to do it. Why? How could he do such an act? ---- I've had enough of that book. }}}}}}
